segments.text: Yahweh, remember David and all his affliction,
how he swore to Yahweh, and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
Surely I will not come into the structure of my house, nor go up into my bed;
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will not give sleep to my eyes, or slumber to my eyelids,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: until I find out a place for Yahweh, a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah. We found it in the field of Jaar.
We will go into his dwelling place. We will worship at his footstool.
segments.speaker: Worshippers

segments.text: Arise, Yahweh, into your resting place, you, and the ark of your strength.
Let your priests be clothed with righteousness. Let your saints shout for joy!
For your servant David’s sake, don’t turn away the face of your anointed one.
Yahweh has sworn to David in truth. He will not turn from it:
I will set the fruit of your body on your throne.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
If your children will keep my covenant, my testimony that I will teach them, 
their children also will sit on your throne forever more.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For Yahweh has chosen Zion. He has desired it for his habitation.
This is my resting place forever. I will live here, for I have desired it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will abundantly bless her provision. I will satisfy her poor with bread.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will also clothe her priests with salvation. Her saints will shout aloud for joy.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will make the horn of David to bud there. I have ordained a lamp for my anointed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will clothe his enemies with shame, but on himself, his crown will shine.
segments.speaker: God
